infotexts.afflictiondescription.bandaged: Protects a Limb from minor ‖color:gui.red‖Infection‖end‖, stabilizes ‖color:gui.red‖Dislocations‖end‖ as well as Arm ‖color:gui.red‖Fractures‖end‖ and increases regeneration of minor Open Wounds. It does however ‖color:gui.red‖reduce‖end‖ movement speed slightly.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.bandageddirty: A ‖color:gui.blue‖Bandage‖end‖ that has become too dirty and should be removed or replaced; otherwise it will promote ‖color:gui.red‖Infections‖end‖. It is still capable of stabilizing ‖color:gui.red‖Dislocations‖end‖ and Arm ‖color:gui.red‖Fractures‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.iced: Application of a ‖color:gui.blue‖Gel Coolant Pack‖end‖ on this Limb has greatly increased the natural healing speed of ‖color:gui.red‖Blunt Force Trauma‖end‖ and is treating ‖color:gui.red‖Internal Bleeding‖end‖ over time. It does however ‖color:gui.red‖reduce‖end‖ movement speed slightly and worsens ‖color:gui.red‖Infections‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.ointmented: A layer of antibiotic gel protects and cleans the wound from any germs. It is very difficult for an ‖color:gui.red‖Infection‖end‖ to develop here.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.firstdegreeburn: The area is red and painful to the touch, and an ‖color:gui.red‖Infection‖end‖ may occur at high severity. ‖color:gui.blue‖Bandages‖end‖, ‖color:gui.blue‖Antibiotic Glue‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.blue‖Antibiotic Ointment‖end‖ can be used to treat the burn if necessary.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.seconddegreeburn: There are very painful blisters littering the affected area. There is a high rate of ‖color:gui.red‖Infection‖end‖ which could lead to life-threatening ‖color:gui.red‖Sepsis‖end‖. ‖color:gui.blue‖Bandages‖end‖, ‖color:gui.blue‖Antibiotic Glue‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.blue‖Antibiotic Ointment‖end‖ can be used to treat the burn.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.thirddegreeburn: The affected area is leathery, stiff and has lost all feeling. ‖color:gui.red‖Infection‖end‖ is almost immediate and thus the onset of ‖color:gui.red‖Sepsis‖end‖ is rapid. ‖color:gui.blue‖Antibiotic glue‖end‖ is the most rapid treatment, though ‖color:gui.blue‖Plastiseal‖end‖ can be used during ‖color:gui.orange‖Open / Close Surgery‖end‖ to replace the skin entirely.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.stimulatedbonegrow: Bone regrowth has been stimulated. To cure ‖color:gui.red‖Bone Death‖end‖, this affliction needs to be on every bodypart.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.fracturedextremity: This limb is broken, and will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Pain‖end‖ until a ‖color:gui.blue‖Plaster Cast‖end‖ is put on the limb or it is surgically repaired.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.dislocation: A bone has been displaced in this limb, causing ‖color:gui.red‖Pain‖end‖ and making movement difficult. A ‖color:gui.blue‖Bandage‖end‖ will restore use of this limb, and a ‖color:gui.blue‖Wrench‖end‖ must be used to relocate the bone.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.fracturedribs: One or more ribs have been broken and unless the torso gets ‖color:gui.green‖Bandaged‖end‖ the structural instability may cause ‖color:gui.red‖Pneumothorax‖end‖. The ribs must be repaired via ‖color:gui.orange‖Osteosynthesis Surgery‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.fracturedskull: The skull has been broken and the structural instability will prevent ‖color:gui.red‖Neurotrauma‖end‖ from naturally decreasing. The skull must be repaired via ‖color:gui.orange‖Osteosynthesis Surgery‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.fracturedneck: The neck has been broken and unless ‖color:gui.green‖Bandaged‖end‖ the structural instability will cause a ‖color:gui.red‖Spinal Cord Injury‖end‖. The neck must be repaired via ‖color:gui.orange‖Osteosynthesis Surgery‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.seizure: Patient is experiencing a full-body seizure. Usually caused by severe ‖color:gui.red‖Acidosis‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Alkalosis‖end‖, a ‖color:gui.red‖Stroke‖end‖, an ‖color:gui.red‖Opiate Overdose‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Radiation Sickness‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.brainremoved: The brain has been surgically removed.
infotexts.afflictioncauseofdeath.brainremoved: Died due to missing a brain
infotexts.afflictiondescription.brainswap: Old brain has been surgically prepared for a new transplant.
infotexts.afflictioncauseofdeath.brainswap: Died due to missing a brain
infotexts.afflictioncauseofdeathself.brainswap: Your brain was in the process of replacement.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.plastercast: Slowly heals ‖color:gui.red‖Fractures‖end‖ on the extremities, though ‖color:gui.red‖reduces‖end‖ movement speed while present. They can be removed using ‖color:gui.blue‖Trauma Shears‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.tourniqueted: ‖color:gui.red‖Bleeding‖end‖ from the artery has been stopped, but blood flow to the extremity is severely reduced - ‖color:gui.red‖Gangrene‖end‖ will occur if not removed in time.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.arterialcut: An artery has been damaged and is causing severe ‖color:gui.red‖Blood Loss‖end‖, apply a ‖color:gui.blue‖Tourniquet‖end‖ as soon as possible!
infotexts.afflictiondescription.gangrene: The extremity has shrunk and turned black. Above 15% this limb cannot be saved anymore and ‖color:gui.red‖must‖end‖ be ‖color:gui.orange‖Amputated‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.tra_amputation: Most of the tissue of the right arm was torn off. This limb can be replaced after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.tla_amputation: Most of the tissue of the left arm was torn off. This limb can be replaced after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.trl_amputation: Most of the tissue of the right leg was torn off. This limb can be replaced after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.tll_amputation: Most of the tissue of the left leg was torn off. This limb can be replaced after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.th_amputation: The head has been violently removed from the body, causing ‖color:gui.red‖instant death‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.sra_amputation: The right arm was surgically amputated.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.sla_amputation: The left arm was surgically amputated.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.srl_amputation: The right leg was surgically amputated.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.sll_amputation: The left leg was surgically amputated.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.luabotomy: Lua isn't running. That means neurotrauma and any other mods that use it won't work. Please read the workshop pages of mods you plan to use.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.modconflict: There is a mod conflict. A different mod is overriding some of the same items Neurotrauma does. Some things may not function correctly. Please adjust your load order.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.tshocktimeout: The patient was left unattended, for some time they won't be aware about having themselves opened.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.spinalcordinjury: The spine has been injured due to a ‖color:gui.red‖Neck Fracture‖end‖, causing ‖color:gui.red‖full-body Paralysis‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Analgesia‖end‖ - though it does not cause ‖color:gui.red‖Unconsciousness‖end‖. It must be surgically repaired using ‖color:gui.blue‖Spinal Cord Implants‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.carotidarterialcut: An artery has been damaged and is causing severe ‖color:gui.red‖Blood Loss‖end‖. You cannot tourniquet the carotid, so it must be surgically fixed!
infotexts.afflictiondescription.increasedheartrate: The patient's heartrate is abnormally high. If this affliction is increasing in strength, potentially lethal ‖color:gui.red‖Fibrillation‖end‖ is occurring; usually due to ‖color:gui.red‖Hypotension‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Hypoxemia‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.fibrillation: The patient's heart is beating abnormally; this could be a sign that potentially lethal ‖color:gui.red‖Fibrillation‖end‖ is occurring; usually due to ‖color:gui.red‖Hypotension‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Hypoxemia‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.cardiacarrest: The patient's heart is not beating. Prevents ‖color:gui.red‖Blood Pressure‖end‖ from normalizing and causes ‖color:gui.red‖Coma‖end‖ if left untreated. The ultimate result of ‖color:gui.red‖Fibrillation‖end‖, as well being caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Hypoxemia‖end‖. The heart must be restarted using a ‖color:gui.blue‖Defibrillator‖end‖, ‖color:gui.blue‖Adrenaline‖end‖ or an ‖color:gui.blue‖AutoPulse‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.heartremoved: The heart has been surgically removed.
infotexts.afflictioncauseofdeath.heartremoved: Died due to missing a heart
infotexts.afflictiondescription.heartswap: Old heart has been surgically prepared for a new transplant.
infotexts.afflictioncauseofdeath.heartswap: Died due to missing a heart
infotexts.afflictioncauseofdeathself.heartswap: Your heart was in the process of replacement.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.skinointmented: A layer of regenerative gel speeds the healing process of burns and blunt force trauma.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.needlec: A needle with an air valve in the chest is preventing ‖color:gui.red‖Pneumothorax‖end‖ from worsening. Applying a Needle to a healthy patient will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Pneumothorax‖end‖ however; requiring the use of a ‖color:gui.blue‖Drainage‖end‖ during ‖color:gui.orange‖Open / Close Surgery‖end‖ to cure it.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.analgesia: Painlessness suppresses certain symptoms and makes surgery possible. At high levels, increases ‖color:gui.green‖Damage- and Stun Resistance‖end‖ while also stabilizing your aim.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.anesthesia: Controlled ‖color:gui.red‖Unconsciousness‖end‖, likely from a drug. It is safe to perform surgery on this person.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.kidneyremoved: The kidneys have been surgically removed.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.kidneyswap: Old kidneys have been surgically prepared for a new transplant.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.liverremoved: The liver has been surgically removed.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.liverswap: Old liver has been surgically prepared for a new transplant.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.respiratoryarrest: The patient is not breathing. A lethal status that will prevent oxygenation of blood. Typically caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Hypoxemia‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Neurotrauma‖end‖, or an ‖color:gui.red‖Opiate Overdose‖end‖. ‖color:gui.green‖Artificial Ventilation‖end‖ from a ‖color:gui.blue‖Surgery Table‖end‖ will counteract the downsides of this affliction.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.hyperventilation: The patient is breathing at an alarmingly rapid rate. Gradually increases non-lethal ‖color:gui.red‖Alkalosis‖end‖, and is usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Hypotension‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Hypoxemia‖end‖ or a ‖color:gui.red‖Pneumothorax‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.hypoventilation: The patient is breathing at an alarmingly slow rate. Gradually increases potentially lethal ‖color:gui.red‖Acidosis‖end‖, which can be prevented by ‖color:gui.green‖Artificial Ventilation‖end‖ from a ‖color:gui.blue‖Surgery Table‖end‖. It is usually caused by ‖color:gui.green‖Analgesia‖end‖. Severe ‖color:gui.red‖Acidosis‖end‖ will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Fibrillation‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.shortnessofbreath: The patients breathing is labored and shallow, usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Cardiac Tamponade‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Pneumothorax‖end‖, or ‖color:gui.red‖Hypoxemia‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.lungremoved: The lungs have been surgically removed.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.lungswap: Old lungs have been surgically prepared for a new transplant.
infotexts.afflictioncauseofdeathself.lungswap: Your lungs were in the process of replacement.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.surgeryincision: An incision has been made by a ‖color:gui.blue‖Scalpel‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.clampedbleeding: Bleeding caused by the incision has been stopped using a ‖color:gui.blue‖Hemostat‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.retractedskin: The skin is being held open by a set of ‖color:gui.blue‖Skin Retractors‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.drilledbones: The bones have been prepared for orthopedic procedures using a ‖color:gui.blue‖Surgical Drill‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.suturedi: An incision has been closed using ‖color:gui.blue‖Sutures‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.sawedbones: The bone has been severed using a ‖color:gui.blue‖Surgical Saw‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.balloonedaorta: The aorta has stopped bleeding, but bloodflow to your extremities and organs is gone.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.traumaticshock: Usually occurring during unsafe surgery conditions, causing potentially lethal ‖color:gui.red‖Hypotension‖end‖. To perform safe surgery a patient must be under the effects of ‖color:gui.green‖Analgesia‖end‖ and near any ‖color:gui.blue‖Bed‖end‖, or be ‖color:gui.red‖Unconscious‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.cough: The patient is coughing periodically. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Cardiac Tamponade‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Lung-‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Heart Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.paleskin: The patients skin is pale. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Hypoxemia‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Blood Loss‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.lightheadedness: The patient is experiencing lightheadedness. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Hypotension‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.blurredvision: The patient is experiencing blurred vision. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Hypotension‖end‖ or a ‖color:gui.red‖Concussion‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.confusion: The patient seems disoriented and confused. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Hypoxemia‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Acidosis‖end‖ or a ‖color:gui.red‖Concussion‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.headache: The patient is experiencing a headache. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Hypoxemia‖end‖, a ‖color:gui.red‖Skull Fracture‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Acidosis‖end‖, a ‖color:gui.red‖Concussion‖end‖ or a ‖color:gui.red‖Stroke‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.legswelling: The patients legs are swollen. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Liver-‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Kidney-‖end‖ or severe ‖color:gui.red‖Heart Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.weakness: The patient is experiencing weakness in the extremities. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Cardiac Tamponade‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Blood Loss‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Acidosis‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.wheezing: The patients breathing is producing a high-pitched noise. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Hemotransfusion Shock‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.vomiting: The patient is vomiting periodically. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Kidney Damage‖end‖, Alcohol consumption or ‖color:gui.red‖Hemotransfusion Shock‖end‖. Vomiting will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Alkalosis‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictioncauseofdeathself.vomiting: You have choked on your own vomit.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.vomitingblood: The patient is vomiting blood periodically. Usually caused by severe ‖color:gui.red‖Internal Bleeding‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Liver Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.fever: The patients body temperature is elevated. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Sepsis‖end‖ or an ‖color:gui.red‖Infected Stomach Cavity‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.abdominaldiscomfor: The patient is experiencing abdominal discomfort. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Liver Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.bloating: The patients abdomen is bloated. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Liver Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.jaundice: The patients skin has turned a sickly shade of yellow. Usually caused by severe ‖color:gui.red‖Liver Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.sweating: The patient is sweating excessively. Usually caused by a ‖color:gui.red‖Heart Attack‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Drug Withdrawal‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.palpitations: The patient is experiencing palpitations. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Alkalosis‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.unconsciousness: The patient is unconscious. Usually caused by severe physical trauma, ‖color:gui.red‖Hypoxemia‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Neurotrauma‖end‖, an ‖color:gui.red‖Aortic Rupture‖end‖ or an ‖color:gui.red‖Opiate Overdose‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.inflammation: The area is red, swollen and warm. Usually caused by an ‖color:gui.red‖Infection‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Foreign Bodies‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.spasm: The patient can't control the erratic behaviour of the limb. Usually caused by severe ‖color:gui.red‖Acidosis‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Alkalosis‖end‖, a ‖color:gui.red‖Stroke‖end‖, an ‖color:gui.red‖Opiate Overdose‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Radiation Sickness‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.craving: A craving for drugs, caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Withdrawal‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.nausea: The skin is clammy and the patient is nauseous. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Kidney Damage‖end‖, a ‖color:gui.red‖Concussion‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Drug Withdrawal‖end‖, or ‖color:gui.red‖Hemotransfusion Shock‖end‖. Vomiting will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Alkalosis‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.onfire: The patient is burning alive! Grab an extinguisher!
infotexts.afflictiondescription.chestpain: The patient is expressing sharp, piercing pain in their chest. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Hemotransfusion Shock‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Fractured Ribs‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.abdominalpain: The patient is expressing persistent pain in their abdomen. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Hemotransfusion Shock‖end‖.
infotexts.afflictiondescription.intensepain: The patient is expressing intense pain in the affected area. Usually caused by ‖color:gui.red‖Dislocations‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Fractures‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.bloodanalyzer: A tool that displays a patient's blood type and immunity. Additionally shows if a patient has ‖color:gui.red‖Blood Loss‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Sepsis‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Acidosis‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Alkalosis‖end‖ or drugs in the bloodstream. If a ‖color:gui.blue‖blank donor card‖end‖ is inserted, it will print the patient's bloodtype onto their ID card. If a ‖color:gui.blue‖blood pack‖end‖ is inserted, any existing ‖color:gui.red‖Sepsis‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Acidosis‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Alkalosis‖end‖ will be shown. The analyzer requires a bit of ‖color:gui.red‖blood‖end‖ to print donor cards.
infotexts.entitydescription.bloodcollector: An empty donor card used by the ‖color:gui.blue‖hematology analyzer‖end‖ to imprint a patient's blood type.
infotexts.entitydescription.emptybloodpack: An empty blood pack for the drawing of blood. The drawn blood will retain a small amount of the donor's ‖color:gui.red‖Acidosis‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Alkalosis‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.red‖Sepsis‖end‖ - if they had any. It is safe to draw a single pack of blood from a patient, provided that they are healthy. On failure, it will draw a ‖color:gui.red‖potentially-lethal‖end‖ amount of blood.
infotexts.entitydescription.bloodpacko_positive: Administerable to: O+, A+, B+, AB+
infotexts.entitydescription.antibloodloss2: A blood pack that can be used on any patient.
infotexts.entitydescription.bloodpacka_negative: Administerable to: A-, A+, AB-, AB+
infotexts.entitydescription.bloodpackb_negative: Administerable to: B-, B+, AB-, AB+
infotexts.entitydescription.o_negativecard: Can take blood from: O- Can give blood to: all of them
infotexts.entitydescription.o_positivecard: Can take blood from: O-, O+ Can give blood to: O+, A+, B+, AB+
infotexts.entitydescription.a_negativecard: Can take blood from: O-, A- Can give blood to: A-, A+, AB-, AB+
infotexts.entitydescription.a_positivecard: Can take blood from: O-, O+, A-, A+ Can give blood to: A+, AB+
infotexts.entitydescription.b_negativecard: Can take blood from: O-, B- Can give blood to: B-, B+, AB-, AB+
infotexts.entitydescription.b_positivecard: Can take blood from: O-, O+, B-, B+ Can give blood to: B+, AB+
infotexts.entitydescription.ab_negativecard: Can take blood from: O-, A-, B-, AB- Can give blood to: AB-, AB+
infotexts.entitydescription.ab_positivecard: Can take blood from: all of them Can give blood to: AB+
infotexts.entitydescription.rarm: A severed right arm. Can be surgically reattached after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖. Severed limbs will slowly decay unless refrigerated, though the condition of a limb does not matter for transplanting purposes. The bones are rich in ‖color:gui.blue‖Calcium‖end‖ and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.larm: A severed left arm. Can be surgically reattached after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖. Severed limbs will slowly decay unless refrigerated, though the condition of a limb does not matter for transplanting purposes. The bones are rich in ‖color:gui.blue‖Calcium‖end‖ and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.rleg: A severed right leg. Can be surgically reattached after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖. Severed limbs will slowly decay unless refrigerated, though the condition of a limb does not matter for transplanting purposes. The bones are rich in ‖color:gui.blue‖Calcium‖end‖ and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.lleg: A severed left leg. Can be surgically reattached after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖. Severed limbs will slowly decay unless refrigerated, though the condition of a limb does not matter for transplanting purposes. The bones are rich in ‖color:gui.blue‖Calcium‖end‖ and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.headta: The remains of a human head that was blown off. The face is mangled and unrecognizable, and it will slowly decay unless refrigerated. The bones are rich in ‖color:gui.blue‖Calcium‖end‖ and are a tasty treat for pets. No, you can't transplant it, don't think about it.
infotexts.entitydescription.headsa: An amputated head in a bag which will slowly decay unless refrigerated. The bones are rich in ‖color:gui.blue‖Calcium‖end‖ and are a tasty treat for pets. No, you can't transplant it, don't think about it.
infotexts.entitydescription.rarmp: A bionic right arm. Can be used to treat amputation, transforming into a biological arm. Can be attached after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.larmp: A bionic left arm. Can be used to treat amputation, transforming into a biological arm. Can be attached after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.rlegp: A bionic right leg. Can be used to treat amputation, transforming into a biological arm. Can be attached after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.llegp: A bionic left leg. Can be used to treat amputation, transforming into a biological arm. Can be attached after ‖color:gui.orange‖Sawing Bones‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.livertransplant: A human liver. To transplant an organ, the old one must be removed and the new one placed in. Organs decay quickly if not refrigerated or in a human, and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.livertransplant_q1: A human liver. To transplant an organ, the old one must be removed and the new one placed in. Organs decay quickly if not refrigerated or in a human, and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.lungtransplant: A set of human lungs. To transplant an organ, the old one must be removed and the new one placed in. Organs decay quickly if not refrigerated or in a human, and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.lungtransplant_q1: A set of human lungs. To transplant an organ, the old one must be removed and the new one placed in. Organs decay quickly if not refrigerated or in a human, and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.kidneytransplant: A human kidney. To transplant an organ, the old one must be removed and the new one placed in. Organs decay quickly if not refrigerated or in a human, and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.kidneytransplant_q1: A human kidney. To transplant an organ, the old one must be removed and the new one placed in. Organs decay quickly if not refrigerated or in a human, and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.hearttransplant: A human heart. To transplant an organ, the old one must be removed and the new one placed in. Organs decay quickly if not refrigerated or in a human, and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.hearttransplant_q1: A human heart. To transplant an organ, the old one must be removed and the new one placed in. Organs decay quickly if not refrigerated or in a human, and are a tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.braintransplant: A human brain. To transplant an organ, the old one must be removed and the new one placed in. The brain will decay even if refrigrated and is extremely fragile, and it is also tasty treat for pets.
infotexts.entitydescription.armlock: This arm has become unusable! \nThis could be because of a ‖color:gui.red‖Dislocation‖end‖, a ‖color:gui.red‖Fracture‖end‖ or an ‖color:gui.red‖Amputation‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.mannitol: A sugar alcohol used to treat a significant amount of ‖color:gui.green‖Neurotrauma‖end‖ and halve its rate of increase. However, it will not work unless the patient has over 70% Blood Pressure and oxygenated blood. Additionally, it will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Heart-‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Kidney Damage‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective and will cause more ‖color:gui.red‖Heart-‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Kidney Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.streptokinase: An enzyme that breaks down blood clots, useful in the treatment of ‖color:gui.green‖Heart Attacks‖end‖. However, it will increase the chance of getting ‖color:gui.red‖Strokes‖end‖ if the user has more than 150% Blood Pressure. Additionally, it is used to treat ‖color:gui.green‖Hemotransfusion Shock‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.immunosuppressant: An immunosuppressant that will reduce a patient's ‖color:gui.green‖Immunity‖end‖. This is useful to prevent ‖color:gui.green‖Organ Rejection‖end‖ or to ‖color:gui.green‖administer incompatible blood types‖end‖ without triggering an immune response. However, a lower immunity will make the patient more susceptible to ‖color:gui.red‖Sepsis‖end‖. If a patient is given an organ transplant but it immediately fails, it has been rejected by the immune system. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Sepsis‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.thiamine: Otherwise known as Vitamin B1. These pills will provide a slight boost to ‖color:gui.green‖Organ Regeneration‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective.
infotexts.entitydescription.ointment: An antiseptic gel that will aid against fighting ‖color:gui.green‖Infected Wounds‖end‖ and relieve minor ‖color:gui.green‖Burns‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective.
infotexts.entitydescription.tourniquet: An item that prevents ‖color:gui.green‖Blood Loss from Arterial Bleeding‖end‖. It cannot be used for the ‖color:gui.red‖Carotid Artery‖end‖. A Tourniqueted limb will experience ‖color:gui.red‖Gangrene‖end‖ over time, and it is removed during surgery. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Blunt Force Trauma‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.drainage: Treats ‖color:gui.green‖Pneumothorax‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Cardiac Tamponade‖end‖ by draining air and fluid respectively. Can be used after ‖color:gui.orange‖Skin Retraction‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.needle: A specialized needle used to depressurize air and fluid buildup, as a first aid option for ‖color:gui.green‖Pneumothorax‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Cardiac Tamponade‖end‖. If the patient does not already have Pneumothorax, it will ‖color:gui.red‖cause one instead‖end‖. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Organ Damage‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Bleeding‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.ringerssolution: An isotonic solution used to increase ‖color:gui.green‖Blood Pressure‖end‖, and additionally causes ‖color:gui.green‖Alkalosis‖end‖. On failure, it has less duration.
infotexts.entitydescription.propofol: A general ‖color:gui.green‖Anesthetic‖end‖. Its effects take place after some time, and will not last very long. Anesthesized patients are ‖color:gui.red‖Unconscious‖end‖ and do not gain ‖color:gui.green‖Traumatic Shock‖end‖ from surgical operations. Usage may come with a variety of non-lethal ‖color:gui.red‖Side Effects‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.gypsum: A powder that can be formed into a Plaster Cast. It is used to encase ‖color:gui.green‖Limb Fractures‖end‖, allowing them to heal. Used after ‖color:gui.blue‖Bandaging‖end‖ the limb. On failure, it is consumed without forming a cast.
infotexts.entitydescription.gelipack: A package of cold gel that will greatly increase the natural healing of ‖color:gui.green‖Blunt Force Trauma‖end‖ and treats ‖color:gui.green‖Internal Bleeding‖end‖ over time. It is ineffective against ‖color:gui.red‖other types of injuries‖end‖ and will worsen ‖color:gui.red‖Infections‖end‖. Effects which increase natural healing speed will not work if the injury is beyond its natural healing threshold. Can be used up to thrice, and will lose effectiveness if not refrigerated. When refrigerated, its condition will regenerate over time. On failure, its chilling duration is decreased.
infotexts.entitydescription.defibrillator: A tool that uses battery power to cure ‖color:gui.green‖Cardiac Arrest‖end‖ or reset ‖color:gui.green‖Fibrillation‖end‖. For curing Cardiac Arrest, use the ‖color:gui.blue‖AED‖end‖ instead if medical skill is below 80. For curing Fibrillation, use the ‖color:gui.blue‖AED‖end‖ instead if medical skill is below 100. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Cardiac Arrest‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.aed: The AED uses battery power for a small chance to cure ‖color:gui.green‖Cardiac Arrest‖end‖ and is guaranteed to reset ‖color:gui.green‖Fibrillation‖end‖. For curing Cardiac Arrest, use the ‖color:gui.blue‖Manual Defibrillator‖end‖ instead if medical skill is above 80.
infotexts.entitydescription.bvm: The Ambu Bag will treat ‖color:gui.green‖Oxygen Low‖end‖ when provided with an oxygen tank and additionally treats ‖color:gui.green‖Respiratory Arrest‖end‖ if provided with an oxygenite tank.
infotexts.entitydescription.autocpr: A machine that occupies a patient's Outerwear slot. Over time, cures ‖color:gui.green‖Cardiac Arrest‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Fibrillation‖end‖. Wearing the AutoPulse will additionally increase ‖color:gui.green‖Blood Pressure‖end‖, which may be useful in an emergency. Remember to take it off when no longer needed! It can also be equipped through the Health UI and unequipped by using a ‖color:gui.blue‖Wrench‖end‖ on the torso.
infotexts.entitydescription.traumashears: A tool used to remove ‖color:gui.green‖Bandages‖end‖ or ‖color:gui.green‖Plaster Casts‖end‖. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Bleeding‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Lacerations‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.wheelchair: A tool that occupies a patient's Outerwear slot. Allows utilizing arms for movement, allowing you to move better with damaged legs.
infotexts.entitydescription.blahaj: A cute plushie that will slightly reduce ‖color:gui.green‖Psychosis‖end‖ each time a patient is hit with it.
infotexts.entitydescription.analgesictank: A tank of analgesics which can be installed on a ‖color:gui.blue‖Surgery Table‖end‖ to provide the patient-laying-down enough Analgesia to perform surgery. It can also be used in an ‖color:gui.blue‖Ambubag‖end‖, but causes extra ‖color:gui.red‖Addiction‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.dialyzer: A filter which can be installed on a ‖color:gui.blue‖Surgery Table‖end‖ to reduce ‖color:gui.green‖Alkalosis‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Acidosis‖end‖ for the patient laying-down. Reducing the afflictions provides a minor risk of contracting ‖color:gui.red‖Sepsis‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.toxfilter: A filter which can be installed on a ‖color:gui.blue‖Surgery Table‖end‖ to treat ‖color:gui.green‖Poisons‖end‖ for the patient laying-down. While it is slower than antidotes, it can treat all vanilla poisons. Treating poisons provides a minor risk of contracting ‖color:gui.red‖Sepsis‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.healthscanner: A tool that uses battery power to display afflictions. It can be aimed at a person for a full-body scan, or administered in the Health UI to show afflictions on a specific body part. Using the scanner causes a minor amount of ‖color:gui.red‖Radiation Sickness‖end‖. Will not display Blood Type, ‖color:gui.red‖Sepsis‖end‖, ‖color:gui.green‖Immunity‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Acidosis‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Alkalosis‖end‖, or Drugs; these afflictions require a ‖color:gui.blue‖Hematology Analyzer‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.medtoolbox: Can store medicine and medical instruments
infotexts.entitydescription.surgerytoolbox: Can store many small items that are used during surgery
infotexts.entitydescription.surgerytoolboxset: Can store many small items that are used during surgery
infotexts.entitydescription.organcrate: A crate to store precious transplant-ready organs and limbs. Organs will decay quickly when left outside, while limbs will decay slower. Organs and limbs will regenerate when refrigerated - but only if the organ is above 90% condition or if the limb is above 95% condition.
infotexts.entitydescription.medstartercrate: Once obtained, this crate will spawn first-aid and surgical necessities.
infotexts.entitydescription.organtoolbox: A container to store precious transplant-ready organs and limbs. Organs will decay quickly when left outside, while limbs will decay slower. Organs and limbs will regenerate when refrigerated - but only if the organ is above 90% condition or if the limb is above 95% condition.
infotexts.entitydescription.bodybag: A bag that occupies a patient or corpse's Outerwear slot. Allows them to be ‖color:gui.green‖dragged at full speed‖end‖, but a living person cannot breathe through the bag, causing ‖color:gui.red‖Oxygen Low‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.stasisbag: A bag that occupies a patient's Outerwear slot, protecting against pressure down to 7,000 meters. This will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Cardiac Arrest‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Respiratory Arrest‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Unconsciousness‖end‖, and ‖color:gui.red‖prevent surgical operations‖end‖, but will ‖color:gui.green‖pause the progression of negative afflictions‖end‖ while worn. When worn, the bag deteriorates by 1% every four seconds.
infotexts.stasisbagequipconfirmation: You will go unconscious. Are you sure you want to equip the stasis bag?
infotexts.entitydescription.advscalpel: A tool used to make ‖color:gui.orange‖Surgical Incisions‖end‖ during surgery. Incisions are the first step in all surgical operations, but will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Blood Loss‖end‖ unless the ‖color:gui.blue‖Hemostat‖end‖ is used afterwards. Additionally, incisions can only be performed if the patient does not feel pain, and patients who become pain-aware during surgery will gain ‖color:gui.red‖Traumatic Shock‖end‖. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Bleeding‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Lacerations‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.advhemostat: A tool used to ‖color:gui.orange‖Clamp Blood Vessels‖end‖ during surgery. The second step in most surgical operations, done after ‖color:gui.orange‖Surgical Incisions‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.advretractors: A tool used to ‖color:gui.orange‖Retract Skin‖end‖ during surgery. Retracting the skin is the third step in most surgical operations, but will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Traumatic Shock‖end‖ if the patient is conscious and not laying on a bed or close to a ‖color:gui.blue‖Surgery Table‖end‖ or hospital bed. Used after ‖color:gui.orange‖Hemostat Clamping‖end‖. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Internal Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.tweezers: A small tool that allows for removing unwanted tissue. After ‖color:gui.orange‖Skin Retraction‖end‖, Tweezers can be used to remove some ‖color:gui.green‖Foreign Bodies‖end‖ or convert ‖color:gui.green‖Blunt Force Trauma‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Internal Damage‖end‖ into ‖color:gui.blue‖Suturable‖end‖ ‖color:gui.red‖Lacerations‖end‖. ‖color:gui.green‖Foreign Bodies‖end‖ can also be removed through open Gunshot Wounds or Deep Tissue Injuries, but this will cause minor ‖color:gui.red‖Traumatic Shock‖end‖. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Internal Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.surgerysaw: A tool used to ‖color:gui.orange‖Saw Bones‖end‖. After ‖color:gui.orange‖Skin Retraction‖end‖, bones can be sawed to remove existing limbs or add new ones. Replacing limbs is required if Gangrene exceeds 15%. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Bleeding‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Internal Damage‖end‖, and ‖color:gui.red‖Lacerations‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.suture: Biodegradeable sutures used to complete surgery. On use, ends surgical operations at any step. Additionally, they can be used to ‖color:gui.orange‖Suture‖end‖ ‖color:gui.green‖Open Wounds‖end‖ closed, and reduce a significant amount of external ‖color:gui.green‖Bleeding‖end‖. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Internal Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.surgicaldrill: A tool used to ‖color:gui.orange‖Drill Bones‖end‖ during surgery. After ‖color:gui.orange‖Skin Retraction‖end‖, bones can be drilled to perform ‖color:gui.orange‖Osteosynthesis Surgery‖end‖ which can fix Fractures. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Bleeding‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Internal Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.osteosynthesisimplants: Titanium implants used to repair Fractures. They can be installed after ‖color:gui.orange‖Drilling Bones‖end‖. In the event of total Bone Death, ‖color:gui.orange‖Osteosynthesis Surgery‖end‖ must be performed on all body parts. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Bleeding‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Internal Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.spinalimplant: Titanium implants used to repair spinal cord injuries. They can be installed after ‖color:gui.orange‖Skin Retraction‖end‖. However, the spinal cord will be injured again if the Neck Fracture is not repaired with ‖color:gui.orange‖Osteosynthesis Surgery‖end‖. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Bleeding‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Internal Damage‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.operatingtable: A portable version of the hospital bed, intended to be installed on submarines. Like the hospital bed, it comes with ‖color:gui.green‖Artificial Ventilation‖end‖. Additionally, it has electrical pins installed that can output a person's vitals. Built-in are slots for placing an ‖color:gui.blue‖Analgesic Tank‖end‖, one of the ‖color:gui.blue‖Filters‖end‖ and a ‖color:gui.blue‖Refrigerated Crate‖end‖. To use the ‖color:gui.blue‖Analgesic Tank‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.blue‖Filters‖end‖ on an unconscious patient, they will have to be forced on the table.
infotexts.entitydescription.multiscalpel: Novelty scalpel. Disabled and needs activation. In the practitioner's hands, a button pad can be utilised to activate the scalpel and switch between between operations: making incisions, extracting organs, removing bandages, or doing malpractice.
infotexts.multiscalpel.bandage: Mode active: Remove bandages and casts / malpractice
infotexts.multiscalpel.speedflex: Mode: Adaptive organ extraction. After ‖color:gui.orange‖Skin Retraction‖end‖, use on the respective limb to remove the following ‖color:gui.blue‖organs‖end‖. To extract ‖color:gui.blue‖organs‖end‖ without transplanting new ones, use on the respective limb twice. Equip in hand to change mode. - Head: ‖color:gui.blue‖Brain‖end‖ - Left Arm: ‖color:gui.blue‖Kidneys‖end‖ - Torso: ‖color:gui.blue‖Liver‖end‖ - Right Arm: ‖color:gui.blue‖Heart‖end‖ - Left Leg: ‖color:gui.blue‖Lungs‖end‖
infotexts.entitydescription.pressuremeds: A medication used to lower ‖color:gui.green‖Blood Pressure‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective.
infotexts.entitydescription.endovascballoon: A balloon inserted into a ruptured aorta. This will prevent ‖color:gui.green‖Blood Loss‖end‖ caused by the rupture, but the clog will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Gangrene‖end‖ over time. It can be installed after a ‖color:gui.orange‖Scalpel Incision‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.medstent: A stent that can be inserted into the aorta to repair a rupture. It must be used after the ‖color:gui.blue‖Endovascular Balloon‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.antiseptic: Used by the ‖color:gui.blue‖Antiseptic Sprayer‖end‖, it is effective against treating ‖color:gui.green‖Infected Wounds‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.organscalpel_liver: A specialized scalpel for the removal of livers.
infotexts.entitydescription.organscalpel_kidneys: A specialized scalpel for the removal of kidneys.
infotexts.entitydescription.organscalpel_lungs: A specialized scalpel for the removal of the lungs.
infotexts.entitydescription.organscalpel_heart: A specialized scalpel for the removal of the heart.
infotexts.entitydescription.organscalpel_brain: A specialized scalpel for the removal of the brain.
infotexts.entitydescription.alienblood: Blood extracted from a Europan lifeform. It is ‖color:gui.red‖incompatible‖end‖ with human blood.
infotexts.entitydescription.antibloodloss1: A sodium chloride infusion used to increase ‖color:gui.green‖Blood Pressure‖end‖, and additionally causes ‖color:gui.green‖Acidosis‖end‖. On failure, it has less duration.
infotexts.entitydescription.antibleeding1:
Basic bandages, useful in the treatment of ‖color:gui.green‖Bleeding‖end‖, and increases the natural healing of ‖color:gui.green‖minor Burns‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖wounds‖end‖. Additionally, being bandaged stabilizes ‖color:gui.green‖Fractures‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Dislocations‖end‖. Effects which increase the speed of natural healing do not work if the injury is beyond its natural healing threshold. On failure, it is less effective. Stabilization prevents: 
- Arm fractures from reaching 100% 
- Neck fractures from causing spinal injury and internal damage 
- Rib fractures from causing pneumothorax 
- Dislocations from causing internal damage 
- Arm dislocations from locking 
- And restores the function of dislocated legs
infotexts.entitydescription.antibleeding2: A synthetic skin with hemostatic properties able to effectively seal ‖color:gui.green‖bleeds‖end‖, and is a good treatment against ‖color:gui.green‖minor Burns‖end‖ while increasing the natural healing of ‖color:gui.green‖Wounds‖end‖. Additionally, stabilizes ‖color:gui.green‖Fractures‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Dislocations‖end‖. Finally, they can be used to treat ‖color:gui.green‖Third-degree Burns‖end‖ when used after ‖color:gui.orange‖Skin Retraction‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective.
infotexts.entitydescription.opium: A relatively mild ‖color:gui.blue‖Opioid‖end‖ ‖color:gui.green‖Analgesic‖end‖ obtained from aquatic poppy. Most commonly used to manufacture Morphine and Fentanyl. On failure, it is less effective and there is a greater risk of ‖color:gui.red‖Addiction‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Overdose‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.antidama1: A powerful ‖color:gui.blue‖Opioid‖end‖ ‖color:gui.green‖Analgesic‖end‖, but overuse will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Addiction‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Overdose‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective and there is a greater risk of ‖color:gui.red‖Addiction‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Overdose‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.antidama2: A very potent ‖color:gui.blue‖Opioid‖end‖ ‖color:gui.green‖Analgesic‖end‖, but overuse will cause ‖color:gui.red‖Addiction‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Overdose‖end‖. Analgesia will not only prevent ‖color:gui.green‖pain‖end‖, but will also reduce ‖color:gui.green‖damage taken‖end‖ and increase ‖color:gui.green‖stun resistance‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective and there is a greater risk of ‖color:gui.red‖addiction‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖overdose‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.antinarc: An opioid antagonist for the treatment of opiate ‖color:gui.green‖Withdrawal‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Overdose‖end‖. Additionally, it will reduce ‖color:gui.green‖Analgesia‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective and may cause a short ‖color:gui.red‖Coma‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.antibiotics: An antibiotic that acts against a wide range bacteria, making it an effective treatment for ‖color:gui.green‖Sepsis‖end‖ and to a lesser extent, ‖color:gui.green‖Husk Infection‖end‖. However, its broad nature causes ‖color:gui.red‖Organ Damage‖end‖. Used as an ingredient in multiple medical compounds. On failure, it is less effective.
infotexts.entitydescription.adrenaline: A naturally occurring hormone. For a short amount of time, it will raise the patient's ‖color:gui.green‖Blood Pressure‖end‖, increase ‖color:gui.green‖Melee Damage‖end‖, halve ‖color:gui.green‖Fibrillation Rate‖end‖, prevent ‖color:gui.green‖Pain‖end‖, and prevent the limb locking and slowdowns from ‖color:gui.green‖Fractures‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Dislocations‖end‖. Upon use, it will also cure ‖color:gui.green‖Cardiac Arrest‖end‖, remove ‖color:gui.green‖Stun‖end‖, and briefly reduce ‖color:gui.green‖incoming Stuns‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.captainspipe: For calming the nerves when the sea is stormy. It is hard on the lungs, though.
infotexts.entitydescription.cigar: A submariner's life is mentally taxing, but perhaps this exquisitely crafted cigar can help you soothe your nerves a bit. It is hard on the lungs, though.
infotexts.entitydescription.liquidoxygenite: A ‖color:gui.red‖volatile‖end‖ syringe of oxygenite that can be injected, forcefully ‖color:gui.green‖oxygenating the blood‖end‖ for a short period of time. However, its toxicity causes slight ‖color:gui.red‖Organ Damage‖end‖. On failure, oxygenation is ‖color:gui.red‖momentary‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖organs are further harmed‖end‖. Hypoxemia damages the brain and organs, and is the main cause of death resulting from combat injuries.
infotexts.entitydescription.deusizine: A stimulant that increases ‖color:gui.green‖muscular endurance‖end‖ and can be used in the treatment of ‖color:gui.green‖Internal Damage‖end‖, ‖color:gui.green‖Oxygen Deprivation‖end‖, and ‖color:gui.green‖Blood Loss‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective.
infotexts.entitydescription.antibleeding3: An antibiotic glue with hemostatic properties. Seals ‖color:gui.green‖Bleeding‖end‖ wounds almost immediately and prevents them from getting ‖color:gui.green‖Infected‖end‖. Also effective against ‖color:gui.green‖Burns‖end‖. On failure, it may cause ‖color:gui.red‖mobile clots‖end‖ in the bloodstream.
infotexts.entitydescription.meth: A potent nervous system stimulant, temporarily boosting the user's ‖color:gui.green‖muscular speed‖end‖. However, it is ‖color:gui.red‖addictive‖end‖, slightly damages ‖color:gui.red‖Organs‖end‖, and will cause some ‖color:gui.red‖Neurotrauma‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective, more ‖color:gui.red‖addicting‖end‖, and will cause increased ‖color:gui.red‖damage‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.steroids: Temporarily boosts ‖color:gui.green‖muscular strength‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖endurance‖end‖. It is addictive. On failure, it is less effective and more ‖color:gui.red‖addicting‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.hyperzine: An extremely potent muscle stimulant for those moments when you gotta go fast. Boosts ‖color:gui.green‖muscular strength‖end‖, ‖color:gui.green‖endurance‖end‖, and ‖color:gui.green‖speed‖end‖, and reduces ‖color:gui.green‖stun susceptibility‖end‖. It is ‖color:gui.red‖addictive‖end‖ and causes a small amount of ‖color:gui.red‖Neurotrauma‖end‖. On failure, it is more ‖color:gui.red‖addictive‖end‖ and causes more ‖color:gui.red‖brain damage‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.antipsychosis: A strong ‖color:gui.green‖antipsychotic‖end‖ drug, effective against treatment of ‖color:gui.green‖Alcohol Withdrawal‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective.
infotexts.entitydescription.antiparalysis: A cure for ‖color:gui.green‖Paralysis‖end‖ and prematurely ends ‖color:gui.green‖Anesthesia‖end‖. Excessive use may cause ‖color:gui.red‖Psychosis‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective and causes more ‖color:gui.red‖Psychosis‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.tonicliquid: A medical solution that's mildly effective as a treatment for ‖color:gui.green‖Internal Damage‖end‖, but is more commonly used to ‖color:gui.green‖increase the duration‖end‖ of bloodstream and performance-enhancing drugs.
infotexts.entitydescription.nitroglycerin: A highly unstable liquid that may explode when subjected to heat or physical shock. When administered to a patient, it will cause vasodilation and decrease ‖color:gui.green‖Blood Pressure‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.pomegrenadeextract: Concentrated pomegrenade juice. It offers minor ‖color:gui.green‖wound-healing‖end‖ properties, including that of ‖color:gui.green‖Sutured Wounds‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective.
infotexts.entitydescription.combatstimulantsyringe: A highly advanced stimulant that makes the user rapidly recover from ‖color:gui.green‖Damage‖end‖, ‖color:gui.green‖Burns‖end‖, and ‖color:gui.green‖Blood Loss‖end‖. In addition, the user will rapidly coagulate ‖color:gui.green‖external‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Internal Bleeding‖end‖. It is ‖color:gui.red‖addictive‖end‖. On failure, it is more ‖color:gui.red‖addicting‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.ethanol: Medical alcohol used as an ingredient in the manufacture of various medicines. Drinking while on duty is not advised, and binge-drinking is hard on the liver.
infotexts.entitydescription.rum: A fine liquor made from raptor bane or pomegrenade juice. Some purists insist real rum is made from sugarcane, but good luck finding any of that on Europa. Its potency is sufficient to damage early ‖color:gui.green‖Husk Infections‖end‖, but binge-drinking is hard on the liver.
infotexts.entitydescription.divingknife: Stabs, pokes, cuts and slashes. Also can be used to remove old ‖color:gui.green‖Bandages‖end‖. On failure, causes ‖color:gui.red‖Lacerations‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.red‖Bleeding‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.raptorbaneextract: Concentrated raptorbane juice. Mildly toxic for humans, catastrophically toxic for mudraptors. It will induce vomiting in humans, making it a decent treatment for ‖color:gui.green‖Acidosis‖end‖. On failure, it does not induce vomiting.
infotexts.entitydescription.proteinbar: May contain traces of galena. Treats some external ‖color:gui.green‖Damage‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Burns‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.stabilozine: Used as an ingredient in the manufacture of various medicines. On its own, can be used to slow down the effects of ‖color:gui.green‖poisons‖end‖ and ‖color:gui.green‖Radiation Sickness‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective.
infotexts.entitydescription.hallucinogenicbufotoxi: Hallucinogenic toxin secreted by some Europan species. Serves as an ‖color:gui.green‖Anti-Paralysant‖end‖ in a pinch.
infotexts.entitydescription.cyanide: A highly toxic chemical that causes a rapid death once a sufficiently large dose has been administered. Can be used as a risky method for eliminating ‖color:gui.green‖Husk Infections‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.europabrew: A double-edged chemical brew. While it activates the immune system to moderately treat ‖color:gui.green‖poisons‖end‖, it also significantly weakens the target, making them more susceptible to all ‖color:gui.red‖Damage‖end‖, ‖color:gui.red‖Burns‖end‖, and ‖color:gui.red‖Husk Infection‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.sufforin: A stealthy neurotoxin, undetectable in early stages. Causes a loss of vision and, ultimately, death. Can be used as a risky method for eliminating ‖color:gui.green‖Husk Infections‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.antirad: Slows down cellular degradation caused by radiation. On failure, it is less effective.
infotexts.entitydescription.calyxanide: An antiparasitic drug used in the treatment of ‖color:gui.green‖Husk Infections‖end‖. On failure, it is less effective.
infotexts.entitydescription.extinguisher: A handheld carbon dioxide extinguisher. Also puts out flaming people.
infotexts.entitydescription.divingsuit: An atmospheric diving suit capable of withstanding the immense pressure under Europa's crust. Only offers adequate protection up to the depth of 4,000 meters—beyond this point, an Abyss or Combat suit is required. Vacuum-sealed suits such as these will easily smother flames.
infotexts.entitydescription.repairpack: A makeshift multi-tool, capable of quickly correcting both mechanical and electrical problems. Like the ‖color:gui.blue‖Wrench‖end‖, they can be used to fix ‖color:gui.green‖Dislocations‖end‖. On failure, results in a ‖color:gui.red‖Fracture‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.wrench: Makes mechanical repairs possible. It is also used to fix ‖color:gui.green‖Dislocations‖end‖, which only requires half the skill to do if the patient does not feel pain. Failing to fix a Dislocation will result in a ‖color:gui.red‖Fracture‖end‖, however.
infotexts.entitydescription.syringegun: Launches syrettes containing drugs or poisons. Doesn't seem to be compatible with ‖color:gui.blue‖Opioids‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.advancedsyringegun: Launches syrettes containing drugs or poisons. Has a higher capacity and integrated health scanner. Doesn't seem to be compatible with ‖color:gui.blue‖Opioids‖end‖.
infotexts.entitydescription.skinaid: A potent regenerative gel that promotes natural healing of most trauma on the surface.
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fracturedneck_1: I think I broke my neck!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fracturedneck_2: My neck just made a loud crunching noise!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.carotidarterialcut_1: Blood is gushing out of my neck!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.carotidarterialcut_2: I'm bleeding heavily from my neck!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.arterialcut_1: Blood is gushing out of one of my limbs!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.arterialcut_2: I'm bleeding heavily from one of my limbs!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.paleskin_2: I feel cold and my skin is discoloring!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.confusion_2: I'm so lost.. what's going on?
infotexts.npcdialogsym.abdominalpain_2: Something in my abdomen hurts a lot!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.abdominalpain_3: I'm having pain in my abdomen!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.inflammation_1: One of my wounds is really warm!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.inflammation_2: One of my wounds is swelling up!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.inflammation_3: One of my wounds is looking very red!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.gangrene_1: One of my limbs is turning black!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.gangrene_2: I'm losing feeling in one of my limbs!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.gangrene_3: I think one of my limbs is rotting!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fever_1: I feel like I'm burning up!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fever_3: I feel warm, yet I keep shivering!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.headache_3: Does anyone have an Ibuprofen? I have a headache.
infotexts.npcdialogsym.headache_4: I have a throbbing pain in my head!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fracturedribs_1: I think I broke a few ribs!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fracturedribs_2: My chest made a loud cracking noise!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fracturedribs_3: My ribs hurt when I breathe!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fracturedskull_1: My skull made a loud cracking noise!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fracturedskull_2: Something is wrong with my head!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fracturedskull_3: I think I just broke my skull!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fracturedextremity_1: I think I broke one of my limbs!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fracturedextremity_2: One of my limbs just made a loud cracking noise!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.fracturedextremity_3: I can't move a limb properly!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.dislocation_1: I think I dislocated one of my limbs!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.dislocation_2: One of my limbs just popped out of the socket!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.dislocation_3: I can't move a limb properly!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.chestpain_2: I feel a painful pressure in my chest!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.chestpain_3: I have a painful feeling in my chest!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.weakness_2: It feels like my muscles won't work..
infotexts.npcdialogsym.shortnessofbreath_2: It feels like I can't breathe!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.shortnessofbreath_3: Something is wrong with my breathing!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.legswelling_2: My leg is way bigger than usual!
infotexts.npcdialogsym.craving_2: I could really use a pick-me-up..
infotexts.npcdialogsym.palpitations_2: My heart is beating faster than usual!
infotexts.ntgui_resetconfirm_body: Are you sure you want to reset the Neurotrauma settings to their default values?
infotexts.ntgui_defaultmessage_config: Server config can only be changed by host or authorized clients.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_calculations: Runs calculations that are necessary for the functionality of the mod. Shouldn't be disabled unless there is borderline unplayable desynchronisation and lag, in which case it might help with a bit.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_vanillaskillcheck: Changes the chance to succeed a lua skillcheck from skill/requiredskill to 100-(requiredskill-skill))/100 .
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_disablebotalgorithms: Prevents bots from attempting to treat afflictions.\nThis is desireable, because bots suck at treating things for the current moment.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_organrejection: When transplanting an organ, there is a chance that the organ gets rejected.\nThe higher the patients immunity at the time of the transplant, the higher the chance.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_fracturesremovecasts: When receiving damage that would cause a fracture, remove plaster casts on the limb
infotexts.ntconfigname_consentrequiredextra: NPCs consent requirement to medical interactions
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_consentrequiredextra: Integrated consent required mod.\nIf enabled, NPCs outside of your team or submarine mission will get aggravated by medical interactions.
infotexts.ntconfigname_creaturenofalldamage: Excluded creatures that abuse the fall damage mechanic
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_creaturenofalldamage: An abuse of fall damage is commonly shown by creatures with heavy or ridicilous knockback, that at worst will instakill or stunlock you.\nYou can add or remove creatures to customize this list to your liking. Use debug command `nt_listcreatures` to list the SpeciesName of the creature you are patching in your game.\nReport other creatures that abuse fall damage to the discord server to improve this default list.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_enablecoloredscanner: Enable colored health scanner text messages.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_lowmedthreshold: Where the Low progress color ends and Medium progress color begins.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_medhighthreshold: Where the Medium progress color ends and High progress color begins.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_namecolor: Scanner text color for player names.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_lowcolor: Scanner text color for afflictions that have low progress.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_medcolor: Scanner text color for afflictions that have medium progress.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_highcolor: Scanner text color for afflictions that have high progress.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_vitalcolor: Scanner text color for vital afflictions (Arterial bleed, Traumatic amputation).
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_removalcolor: Scanner text color for removed organs (Heart removed, leg amputation).
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_customcolor: Scanner text color for the custom category.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_vitalcategory: You can add or remove afflictions to customize this list to your liking.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_removalcategory: You can add or remove afflictions to customize this list to your liking.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_customcategory: You can add or remove afflictions to customize this list to your liking.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_ignoredcategory: Afflictions added to this category will be ignored by the health scanner.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_hardmodeaorticruptur: Enable the Medical Stent and Aortic Balloon as the only method to fix Aortic Rupture.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_openclosetamponade: Enable the closing with sutures as the only method to fix Cardiac Tamponade.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_donitroprusside: Allow Sodium Nitroprusside to be fabricated, bought and found.
infotexts.ntconfigdescription_doorganscalpels: Allow Organ scalpels and Surgery Box Scalpel set to be fabricated, bought and found.
